theme.title: Rotation: Value/Defensives → Growth
theme.summary: Technology (+2.35%) and Consumer Discretionary (+1.48%) led the market today, while Consumer Staples (-1.83%) and Energy (-1.36%) brought up the rear. Growth sectors outpaced the rest, averaging +1.28% versus -0.76% for defensives and +0.41% for value, consistent with risk appetite favoring higher-beta names. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.80%, Dow +0.28%, Nasdaq +1.20% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps bucked the broader trend, with the Russell 2000 up +1.40%, outperforming large caps. The VIX fell 5.27% to 15.99, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 1.59% to $4,136.70, crude oil was little changed at $71.85, and bitcoin gained 1.57% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
